title: npm run onboard boots the Work HQ into a guided wizard that scaffolds the personal OS and hands the constitution to the AI
context: Onboarding is the weakest part of the current template: onboarding.html is a handsome static explainer, but it explains rather than guides — a new user still faces empty context files, an unconfigured assistant, and no confirmation that anything works. M5's acceptance bar (clone to first successful standup in under 30 minutes) was met with developer-adjacent testers; the guidance gap is the blocker for less technical users. The os-helper skill already owns chat-side onboarding ('onboard [name]'), including detecting a failed @AGENTS.md import. The question is what npm run onboard runs and how the wizard divides labour with the AI.

options.label: A
options.summary: Enhance the static onboarding.html: add interactive checklists and copy buttons, but keep it a served static page
pros: Smallest build; keeps the existing well-designed page as the base
No server needed — could even keep working from file://
cons: Cannot write files, so the user still hand-creates me.md, active.md, etc. from templates — the core friction survives
Cannot read files, so it can never verify progress or confirm the constitution actually loaded
A second, separate artefact once the Work HQ exists — two UIs to maintain and keep consistent

options.label: B
options.summary: Terminal CLI wizard (inquirer-style prompts) that writes the personal files directly
pros: Tiny dependency footprint; no browser context switch
Direct file writes make scaffolding trivial
cons: A terminal Q&A is the least hospitable medium for the exact audience with the guidance problem — no visuals, no progress overview, no explanatory side panel
Cannot reuse the Work HQ's screens, components, or the Collect & Copy bridge (ADR-P6-003); parallel codebase for one-time use
Long free-text answers (role description, stakeholder map) are miserable to type into a terminal prompt

options.label: C
options.summary: A wizard route inside the Work HQ app: npm run onboard starts the same dev server opening at /onboarding, a stepped flow that scaffolds files and ends with a handoff to the AI
pros: One app, one component library, one input bridge — the wizard is just another consumer of ADR-P6-003's save + Collect & Copy machinery
Server functions let the wizard scaffold the gitignored personal files (me.md, active.md, BACKLOG.md placeholder) from templates/ with the user's answers already filled in

Can verify as it goes: which files exist, whether headers carry Updated dates, and it can coach the assistant-wiring step (chat.useAgentsMdFile, CLAUDE.md shim check) with a copyable verification prompt
Onboarding state persisted as a Work-HQ-native JSON entity (ADR-P6-002), so the wizard is resumable and the Health screen can show 'onboarding incomplete'
cons: Onboarding now requires Node + npm install before first value — the heaviest possible first step for a non-technical user (OQ-002); chat-only onboarding must remain documented as the fallback
Wizard quality bar is high: a broken guided flow is worse than no guided flow
The static onboarding.html becomes redundant as an entry point and needs an explicit fate (OQ-001)

decision: Adopt Option C. npm run onboard starts the Work HQ server and opens /onboarding: an instructional, interview-style wizard — a smart multi-turn form organised in logical phases, explicitly designed to span many sessions (onboarding-state JSON records per-phase completion, so the user can leave and resume where they stopped; per OQ-001). Phases (order amended by ADR-P6-007 — assistant wiring must precede seeding because the bootstrap runs through the assistant): (1) Orientation — intro and intent, absorbing the static content of onboarding.html, which is then deleted (OQ-001: one onboarding surface, no drift); (2) Wire your assistant — the as-is setup assumes GitHub Copilot in the VS Code native chat UI reading AGENTS.md (OQ-003): checklist for chat.useAgentsMdFile / CLAUDE.md shim with a copyable verification prompt ('confirm you can read my constitution and tell me my #1 priority'), the os-helper import-debug steps surfaced on failure, plus a note guiding the user's AI assistant to adapt the setup when their assistant is not Copilot; (3) Seed your profile — path choice per ADR-P6-007: Glean-bootstrap (copy the bootstrap prompt; the assistant researches and writes context/profile.md) or manual interview; (4) Review & correct — seeded path: every profile section rendered as an editable card with Confirmed/Derived/Unknown status, corrections handoff locks the profile in (ADR-P6-007); manual path: the About-you and Your-org interview forms scaffolding context/me.md and context/org.md (committed file: wizard shows the sensitivity-check wording before writing); (5) Right now — top-3 priorities (each with named owner and due date) and blockers scaffolding context/active.md, both paths; (6) First standup — Collect & Copy handoff block inviting the AI to run the inaugural 'morning standup' and os-helper 'onboard' harvest. Labour split: the wizard writes only gitignored personal scaffolds and its own onboarding-state JSON; anything constitution-level (AGENTS.md edits, skill changes) is handed to the AI via the handoff block — the AI remains the sole editor of shared, committed OS files.
consequences: The 30-minute M5 onboarding criterion is re-baselined to include npm install, and the prerequisite step is delegated to the user's AI assistant: the README is written to be assistant-executable, so the user points their assistant at it and the assistant runs the Node.js/npm preflight and first launch (OQ-002). The chat-only path ('onboard' via os-helper) remains documented as the no-Node fallback. onboarding.html's intro/intent content is folded into the Orientation phase and the file is deleted (OQ-001). os-helper gains a wizard-aware mode: when onboarding-state JSON shows wizard completion, chat onboarding skips straight to verification instead of re-interviewing. templates/ becomes a runtime dependency of the wizard (scaffold sources), so template edits must keep placeholder markers intact.
costCapacity: No new recurring cost; wizard runs on the same local server as the Work HQ (ADR-P6-006).
